References

This page collects books, research papers, documentation, and resources used throughout the robotics workspace documentation.

Quick Links

  • Concepts - Key concepts and glossary

  • Troubleshooting - Common issues and solutions

  • user_manual - User manual and guides

Documentation Resources

Wall-E Robot

  • Wall-E Robot - Wall-E variant documentation

  • Wall-E Subsystems - Subsystems overview

Gas Robot Variant

  • Gas Robot Variant - Gas Robot variant documentation

  • Gas Robot Setup - Setup and configuration

  • Gas Robot Subsystems - Advanced subsystems

Shared Platform Documentation

  • Getting Started - Getting started guide

  • Architecture - System architecture

  • Communication Model - Communication protocols

  • ESP32 Controller - Firmware documentation

  • Workflow and Commands - Development workflow

  • WiFi Provisioning - Deployment guide

Foundational Robotics and State Estimation

[1] R. Mahony, T. Hamel, and J.-M. Pflimlin, “Nonlinear Complementary Filters on the Special Orthogonal Group,” IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control, vol. 53, no. 5, pp. 1203-1218, Jun. 2008.

[2] S. O. H. Madgwick, “An efficient orientation filter for inertial and inertial/magnetic sensor arrays,” University of Bristol, Bristol, U.K., Tech. Rep., 2010.

[3] G. Welch and G. Bishop, “An Introduction to the Kalman Filter,” University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill, Chapel Hill, NC, USA, Tech. Rep. TR 95-041, 2006.

[4] S. Thrun, W. Burgard, and D. Fox, Probabilistic Robotics. Cambridge, MA, USA: MIT Press, 2005.

[5] J. L. Crassidis and J. L. Junkins, “Optimal Estimation of Dynamic Systems,” Second Edition. Boca Raton, FL, USA: CRC Press, 2012.

Robotics and Control Textbooks

[6] B. Siciliano, L. Sciavicco, L. Villani, and G. Oriolo, Robotics: Modelling, Planning and Control. London, U.K.: Springer, 2009.

[7] K. J. Astrom and R. M. Murray, Feedback Systems: An Introduction for Scientists and Engineers. Princeton, NJ, USA: Princeton Univ. Press, 2008.

[8] K. J. Astrom and T. Hagglund, Advanced PID Control. Research Triangle Park, NC, USA: ISA, 2006.

[9] J. J. Craig, Introduction to Robotics: Mechanics and Control, Fourth Edition. Hoboken, NJ, USA: Pearson, 2017.

[10] R. M. Murray, Z. X. Li, and S. S. Sastry, A Mathematical Introduction to Robotic Manipulation. Boca Raton, FL, USA: CRC Press, 1994.

SLAM and Navigation

[11] R. C. Smith and P. Cheeseman, “On the representation and estimation of spatial uncertainty,” International Journal of Robotics Research, vol. 5, no. 4, pp. 56-68, 1986.

[12] H. Bailey, “Mobile Robot Localization and Mapping in Dynamic Outdoor Environments,” University of Sydney, Ph.D. dissertation, 2002.

[13] S. Thrun, M. Montemerlo, H. Dahlkamp, D. Stavens, A. Aron, J. Diebel, P. Fong, J. Gale, M. Halpenny, G. Hoffmann, K. Lau, C. Oakley, M. Palatucci, V. Pratt, P. Stang, S. Strohband, C. Dupont, L. E. Jendrossek, C. Koelen, C. Mariyama, M. Marques, M. RunKe, D. Shinoda, J. Stapleton, A. Theriault, A. E. Trepagnier, C. Wang, O. Werling, and J. Ziglar, “Stanley: The robot that won the DARPA Grand Challenge,” Journal of Field Robotics, vol. 23, no. 9, pp. 661-692, 2006.

Software Platforms and Middleware

[14] M. Quigley et al., “ROS: an open-source Robot Operating System,” in ICRA Workshop on Open Source Software, Kobe, Japan, 2009.

[15] M. Macenski et al., “Robot Operating System 2: Design, architecture, and uses in the wild,” Science Robotics, vol. 7, no. 66, 2022.

[16] Open Robotics, “ROS 2 Documentation (Humble),” [Online]. Available: https://docs.ros.org/en/humble/. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[17] Open Robotics, “ROS 2 Navigation,” [Online]. Available: https://docs.ros.org/en/humble/Tutorials/Advanced/Navigation/Navigation.html. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[18] micro-ROS Team, “micro-ROS Documentation,” [Online]. Available: https://micro.ros.org/docs/. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[19] eProsima, “Micro XRCE-DDS Documentation,” [Online]. Available: https://micro-xrce-dds.docs.eprosima.com/. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[20] D. Merkel, “Docker: lightweight Linux containers for consistent development and deployment,” Linux Journal, no. 239, 2014.

[21] Docker, Inc., “Docker Documentation,” [Online]. Available: https://docs.docker.com/. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

Embedded Systems and Hardware

[22] Espressif Systems, “ESP-IDF Programming Guide,” [Online]. Available: https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-idf/. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[23] Espressif Systems, “ESP32 Technical Reference Manual,” [Online]. Available: https://www.espressif.com/en/support/documents/technical-documents. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[24] Espressif Systems, “ESP32-S3 SoC Datasheet,” [Online]. Available: https://www.espressif.com/sites/default/files/documentation/esp32-s3_datasheet_en.pdf. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[25] FreeRTOS, “FreeRTOS Kernel Documentation,” [Online]. Available: https://www.freertos.org/Documentation/RTOS_book.html. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[26] Real Time Engineers Ltd., “FreeRTOS Reference Manual,” [Online]. Available: https://www.freertos.org/Documentation/161204_Fundamentals_EBook_V2.pdf. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

Computer Vision

[27] D. G. Lowe, “Distinctive image features from scale-invariant keypoints,” International Journal of Computer Vision, vol. 60, no. 2, pp. 91-110, 2004.

[28] G. Bradski and A. Kaehler, Learning OpenCV: Computer Vision with the OpenCV Library. Sebastopol, CA, USA: O’Reilly Media, 2008.

[29] Y. LeCun, Y. Bengio, and G. Hinton, “Deep learning,” Nature, vol. 521, no. 7553, pp. 436-444, 2015.

Tool Documentation

[30] Sphinx Documentation, “Sphinx Documentation Generator,” [Online]. Available: https://www.sphinx-doc.org/. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[31] reStructuredText, “reStructuredText Markup Specification,” [Online]. Available: https://docutils.sourceforge.io/rst.html. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[32] Git, “Git Documentation,” [Online]. Available: https://git-scm.com/doc. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].

[33] VS Code, “Visual Studio Code Documentation,” [Online]. Available: https://code.visualstudio.com/docs. [Accessed: May. 7, 2026].
